ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for battered fried chicken wings as a fast-food product; closest match is fried chicken (FOODON_03302050).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coated and fried chicken meat; no specific fast-food wing sub-entry exists. Used as closest defensible analogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT food concept for battered fried chicken wings identified; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of McDonald's McWings consists of 4 battered and deep-fried chicken wing pieces, weighing approximately 200 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, coated, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05307 / FDC ID 331960)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for chicken wing, coated, fried (NDB 05307) used as the closest approved analogue for McDonald's McWings, which are battered and deep-fried chicken wings. McDonald's Singapore does not publish full nutrient panels in an approved primary database. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy per-100g figures for coated fried chicken wing. Minor sodium adjustment applied upward (~10%) to reflect fast-food seasoning levels, consistent with USDA Branded Food category averages for fast-food fried chicken.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed fried chicken and batter coating — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's McWings. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous fried battered chicken products in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Battered fried chicken has a moderate protein and fat content that suppresses postprandial glucose response; the wheat-flour batter contributes modest glycemic carbohydrate. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 15.6) / 100 ≈ 7.0, where carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 7.8 × 2 = 15.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1040 mg per 4-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid McDonald's McWings due to the high sodium content (~1040 mg per serving), which represents approximately 45% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg and can significantly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load and high total fat from deep-frying
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume McWings only occasionally, as the wheat-flour batter contributes glycemic carbohydrate (GL ~7 per serving) and the high fat content from deep-frying may impair insulin sensitivity and contribute to excess caloric intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should strictly limit consumption of McWings, as each serving delivers approximately 10.2 g saturated fat, 0.4 g trans fat, 156 mg cholesterol, and 1040 mg sodium — all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's McWings are battered and deep-fried chicken wing pieces sold at McDonald's restaurants in Singapore, typically served in portions of 4 pieces (approximately 200 g). The wings are coated in a light seasoned wheat-flour batter before being submerged in hot vegetable oil, producing a crispy golden exterior with juicy, tender chicken meat inside. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly spiced, with a satisfying crunch from the batter coating. McWings are classified as an ultra-processed fast-food product and are certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g they provide approximately 289 kcal, 22.5 g protein, 19.2 g fat (of which 5.1 g is saturated), and 7.8 g carbohydrate. A 4-piece serving (~200 g) delivers roughly 578 kcal and 1040 mg sodium, making it a high-calorie, high-sodium food. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category) due to the protein and fat content moderating glucose absorption, though the overall dietary pattern of fast-food consumption is associated with increased cardiometabolic risk. Clinically, McWings are of concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol), and Type 2 Diabetes (caloric density, fat-mediated insulin resistance). They are not suitable as a regular dietary component for any of these conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in McDonald's McWings?
How much sodium in McDonald's chicken wings?

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore does not publish a full nutrient panel in any approved primary database (HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded entry for Singapore market). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy coated fried chicken wing data (NDB 05307), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05307 (chicken wing, coated, fried) as no Singapore-specific approved-source entry exists for McDonald's McWings. Sodium has been adjusted upward by approximately 10% to reflect fast-food seasoning levels consistent with USDA Branded Food category averages. 2. GI is estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous battered fried chicken products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables; no direct GI measurement for McWings exists. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for coated fried chicken — this product contains no meaningful vitamin C. 5. Halal certification confirmed via McDonald's Singapore Halal certification (MUIS-certified). 6. Serving size of 200 g (4 pieces) is estimated based on typical fast-food chicken wing portion weights; McDonald's Singapore does not publish per-piece weights in an approved source. 7. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and all micronutrients except vitamin_d_mcg are sourced from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05307 and are reported as numeric values. 8. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional field survey title 'McWings® (4pc)' to the canonical form 'McDonald's McWings (4 pieces)'.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that vitamin_d_mcg sentinel value (-1) required explicit notes documentation per g3 sentinel rule.

Ensured aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list by adding 'Mai Wei Ji Chi (Mandarin romanization)' to both aliases_by_language.romanized and the flat aliases list, resolving q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage failure.
